data.title: Americas Gold and Silver: Strong Q2 Beat, Operational Momentum and Deleveraging Support Buy Rating and Unchanged $12 Target
data.description:
Analyst Brian Quast of BMO Capital reiterated a Buy rating on Americas Gold and Silver, retaining the price target of C$12.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Brian Quast has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including stronger-than-expected operating performance and reaffirmed guidance. Americas Gold and Silver delivered second-quarter silver output that exceeded both his and the market’s forecasts, with both Cosalá and Galena performing above plan, while production of copper, lead, and antimony also surpassed expectations.

Brian Quast’s rating is based on his view that the company can still meet its full-year production targets, supported by a planned ramp-up at Galena and recent infrastructure upgrades that should reduce operational disruptions. He also highlights that inventory builds and the removal of over US$85 million in future variable debt obligations improve the company’s financial flexibility, and with execution of its optimization strategy, he sees meaningful upside potential, justifying an unchanged $12 target price and a Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $9.75 price target.

data.title: Analyst Reaffirms Buy on Choice Properties, Citing Solid FFO, NOI Growth and Strategic $5B First Capital Acquisition
data.description:
Choice Properties Real Estate Investment, the Real Estate sector company, was revisited by a Wall Street analyst yesterday. Analyst Tom Callaghan from BMO Capital maintained a Buy rating on the stock and has a C$17.00 price target.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Tom Callaghan has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors that underscore both earnings visibility and balance sheet flexibility. He notes that Choice Properties’ recent quarter showed steady funds-from-operations and solid same-property NOI growth, particularly from the industrial segment, while management reaffirmed its 2026 growth targets, indicating confidence in the medium-term outlook.

He also highlights the strategic rationale of the planned $5.0B First Capital asset acquisition, which should improve the quality and growth profile of the retail portfolio and support valuation multiple expansion over time. Although leverage is expected to rise following the transaction, he views this as manageable given the REIT’s historically conservative capital structure, proven deleveraging track record, and a valuation that, while toward the higher end of its range, is justified by a strong retail environment, a sizable high-quality portfolio, and capable management.

PPRQF’s price has also changed slightly for the past six months – from C$15.700 to C$15.780, which is a 0.51% increase.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Edwards with Neutral Risk-Reward and Reiterated $85 Price Target Amid Pending TAVR Catalysts
data.description:
Canaccord Genuity analyst William Plovanic maintained a Hold rating on Edwards Lifesciences yesterday and set a price target of $85.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

William Plovanic has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors tied to Edwards’ current performance and its risk‑reward profile. The company delivered a strong quarterly beat in both TAVR and TMTT, and management raised full‑year revenue guidance, but the increase largely mirrors the recent upside rather than signaling a fundamentally higher growth trajectory.

At the same time, key catalysts for the core TAVR business, including the final CMS coverage decision and the PROGRESS trial outcome, are still pending and could materially influence the long‑term outlook. Management’s cautious stance on the second half of the year, tougher TAVR comparisons, FX‑driven margin pressure, and a valuation that already discounts much of the expected growth collectively support maintaining a neutral, Hold recommendation and an $85 price target.

According to TipRanks, Plovanic is a 2-star analyst with an average return of 0.0% and a 40.88% success rate. Plovanic covers the Healthcare sector, focusing on stocks such as TransMedics Group, Atricure, and Artivion.

data.title: AMD Buy Rating Reaffirmed as AI Infrastructure Momentum and Strategic Partnerships Support Unchanged $665 Price Target
data.description:
Rosenblatt Securities analyst Kevin Cassidy maintained a Buy rating on Advanced Micro Devices today and set a price target of $665.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Kevin Cassidy has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, centered on AMD’s strong execution and expanding opportunity in AI infrastructure. He highlights that AMD launched its Helios AI rack system and Venice 2nm server CPU on time, with substantial customer commitments and flexible chiplet-based SKUs that can serve a wide range of data center workloads, reinforcing AMD’s performance leadership.

Cassidy also points to AMD’s sharply higher total addressable market estimates in data center AI accelerators and CPUs through 2030, as well as strategic partnerships with Microsoft, Anthropic, and Cerebras that validate Helios as ready for large-scale, real-world deployment. These collaborations, combined with a visible roadmap for GPU and CPU performance gains into 2027–2028 and an unchanged price target of $665, underpin his conviction that AMD’s growth and earnings power justify a Buy recommendation.

In another report released today, Bank of America Securities also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $620.00 price target.

data.title: Neal Dingmann Reiterates Buy on NioCorp, Citing Elk Creek De-Risking, EXIM Funding Prospects, and $12 Fair Value Upside
data.description:
William Blair analyst Neal Dingmann has maintained their bullish stance on NB stock, giving a Buy rating on July 15.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Neal Dingmann has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors tied to NioCorp’s progress on the Elk Creek Critical Minerals Project and its financing pathway. He views the upcoming completion of the updated feasibility study and the anticipated EXIM Bank funding as key milestones that could unlock full-scale construction and materially de-risk the project’s execution.

He also highlights the project’s authorized preconstruction spending, advancing mine portal work, and the company’s active efforts to secure additional offtake agreements as important indicators of momentum. In his view, Elk Creek’s planned production of ferroniobium, scandium, and titanium—backed by growing U.S. government support and its role in critical supply chains—supports a fair value estimate of $12 per share, implying substantial upside from current levels.

In another report released on July 15, H.C. Wainwright also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a $10.00 price target.

data.title: Analyst Raises Mullen Group Price Target to $32, Reiterates Buy on Record Results and Project Upside
data.description:
John Gibson CFA, an analyst from BMO Capital, maintained the Buy rating on Mullen Group. The associated price target is C$32.00.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

John Gibson CFA has given his Buy rating due to a combination of factors, including Mullen Group’s strong quarterly performance and improving industry backdrop. The company delivered record revenue and a notable jump in adjusted EBITDA, with its Less-Than-Truckload segment achieving margins above 20%, signaling durable profitability as freight markets tighten.

Gibson also highlights the potential upside from large-scale projects, where Mullen is increasing fleet investments to capture opportunities such as the Alaska LNG development and proposed Canadian pipelines. Reflecting higher EBITDA forecasts and embedded optionality from potential project wins, he raised his target price to $32, implying a higher valuation multiple supported by an improving freight cycle and anticipated earnings growth through 2028.

In another report released yesterday, RBC Capital also reiterated a Buy rating on the stock with a C$30.00 price target.

MTL’s price has also changed dramatically for the past six months – from C$16.770 to C$26.800, which is a 59.81% increase.

data.title: Analyst Maintains Hold on Vita Coco: Solid Headline Quarter, Modest Core Outlook Improvement and Persistent Private-Label Mix
data.description:
Needham analyst Gerald Pascarelli has reiterated their neutral stance on COCO stock, giving a Hold rating yesterday.

Claim 55% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock powerful investing tools with TipRanks Premium to make smarter, more confident investment decisions
  • Subscribe to TipRanks Smart Investor Newsletter, and discover new investing opportunities with data-backed stock picks

Gerald Pascarelli has given his Hold rating due to a combination of factors, starting with a quarter that looked solid on the surface but was more mixed beneath. Overall results exceeded expectations, yet core Vita Coco Americas volumes lagged, with most of the outperformance tied to private-label products, a pattern he expects could persist and limit upside from the branded business.

He also notes that management raised guidance, but once the contribution from the Copra acquisition is stripped out, the improvement in the underlying outlook is relatively modest, especially given already high investor expectations and the recent share pullback. While he views consumer demand as healthy, guidance as likely conservative, and Copra as a low-risk deal, he flags emerging cost pressures such as higher freight as potential headwinds, leading him to lift estimates but maintain a balanced, non-committal Hold stance.

Pascarelli covers the Consumer Defensive sector, focusing on stocks such as Turning Point Brands, Constellation Brands, and Celsius Holdings. According to TipRanks, Pascarelli has an average return of -5.7% and a 33.02% success rate on recommended stocks.
data.author: TipRanks Auto-Generated Intelligence Newsdesk
